old_claim.note: Mass Media (Newspapers) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because the scoped node is source-backed by motion-picture film production, distribution, and exhibition firms; newspapers may publicize programs but are not evidenced as a prerequisite for distributing film prints or operating cinema networks.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica describes Pathé and Gaumont as early motion-picture production/distribution companies and does not ground cinema distribution in newspaper infrastructure.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source explains early cinema distribution through film companies and motion-picture output, not newspaper media.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Newspapers are plausible advertising context, but the inspected scope is film-print and exhibition distribution; publicity channels should not be modeled as direct prerequisites without source support.

ontology_before: Industrial Cinema Distribution depended directly on mass media newspapers, implying newspaper circulation enabled the film distribution network.
ontology_after: Industrial Cinema Distribution is grounded directly in motion-picture production/projection; newspaper publicity is left as optional advertising context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: industrial_cinema_distribution no longer directly depends on mass_media_newspapers.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to newspaper film listings or newspaper advertising for cinemas.
A stronger source shows early cinema distribution networks required newspaper circulation to operate.
A separate cinema_advertising node is added where newspaper advertising is a direct scoped prerequisite.
short_reason: Newspapers are publicity context, not a sourced direct prerequisite for cinema distribution.
